channel.title: Comments for page "Going Home"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Going Home"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am so utterly confused. So 049 sucks the blood out of a tiny mosquito, using a syringe which the needle would obviously be far too big to prod a tiny mosquito with anyway, to be completely honest and replaces the blood with something else. Then the mosquito is brought to life but now it is TALKING and it thinks it’s some girl named Leslie who’s been dead for 200 years!? I am so confused. What is going on? I have so many questions and no idea where to find the answer. I don’t even know if I wanna find out the answer because this is also bizarre.

So 049 not only figures out how to bring the dead back to life without them being zombies, but he somehow learns how to transplant past souls into insignificant insects? I am so lost here. Where does he get the ability to transplant human souls/consciousness? Where did he even get the soul from? How did he acquire this new power?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *sniffle, sniffle* POOR BABY waiting for her friend who's never gonna come home ;n; so saaad… but so beautiful. Wonderfully written T-T make me cry

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Leslie as a character is so sweet, but her backstory is so sad. And still, she's a mosquito.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just took the time to reread this on a whim. I love this so much more than I remembered, I actually cried. I suppose when I’m not competing with you for upvotes that it’s easier to enjoy the nuanced writing, huh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The doctor FINALLY perfected his cure to where it wouldn’t make the subject a zombie, and now there’s no more humans left to cure. That must be frustrating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aww Leslie ;-;
I don't even know this scp but she's so precious, I love her so much
I would hug her if I wasn't afraid of accident killing her.
UgH

Anyways, the story was fantastic as usual, and I feel blessed just by reading it. I will always enjoy reading whatever you write, Jade :D I've said it before, but you are a god-tier author.

Cheers!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im really glad I went back to reread all these tales. This is fantastic. I especially love the detailed depiction of the surgery he performed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aaaaaaah I’m in pain. My feelings are critically wounded. I’m not coming back from this one. The dialogue, the twist, the characters, it’s all so much! Take your +1 and leave my broken heart!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, my goodness. This was unexpectedly moving.

item.title: First entry in the OCT
item.content:encoded:
Sincere thanks to Othello the Cat for looking the piece over, and for letting me have the pleasure of writing for the lovely Leslie.

Thank you to my critters: Uncle Nicolini, Cyantreuse, Magnadeus, and to everyone else who gave their input on the tale. I appreciate all of you.
